title: Chopper’s Fear Surfaces
description: On pages 9–11, this part focuses on chopper’s fear surfaces, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Chopper’s Fear Surfaces. The story turns inward to Chopper’s isolation, his fear of humans, and the painful memories behind his bond with Dr. Hiriluk. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: 45... 3, 4, 5... so thousand bell, huh...

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Hmph! Being stingy over a Measly 50,000!? Cheap son of a...

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: And so what if I accidentally burned down your house? Stop your damn complaining!

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Damn it...

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Caugh!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Quick Doctor Dr. HillUK

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: That does it, I'm calling the MD-20! Who cares if you have to grovel to Wapol!? It's better than dying!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Daddy!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: pant... Dammit... pant... just the thought of being indebted to that despicable monster is...

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: There's no need for that!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: What!?

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: I'll examine him.

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: Dr. Hiriluk!? From the chimney!?

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Don't worry, I place my patient's health first and foremost.

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: N-No, it's fine, really!

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: I... I've already decided to call the MD-20 in-stead so...

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Don't worry, you're in good hands now.

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: I've just developed a brilliant all- purpose drug so consider yourself lucky.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: H-Honey, I'll go call the guards!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Don't be stupid, woman!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Gya- AAA!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Moooooommmm!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Just a tranquil- izer. She won't die.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I'll pay how- ever much you want so please just leave my home!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: What do you take me for, a CROOK!? I don't take any money from my patients.

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: All I want to do is help you!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Then just leave! That's the best way you can help me!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: I've got a wife and kid! I can't die now!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: DESCRIPTION (SPSPESSE)

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Hmm?

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: That's odd...

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Was I supposed to administer that intra-venously?

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Or did I maybe get the ingredients mixed up...? But I'm sure that frog extract should've done the trick...!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Damn it, looks like another failure.

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: WAAA- AAHH! Mommy! Daddy!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: S-Sorry kiddo... I really didn't intend for that to happen...

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: I think I heard the gun- shot coming from here!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Crap, it's the guards!

summary: This part highlights chopper’s fear surfaces and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
